R3 Serial0/1/0 IP 10.0.0.9/30 (link to R1) and Serial0/1/1 IP 10.0.0.6/30 (link to R2).

root_causes.cause: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is manually configured to 100Mbps half-duplex with autonegotiation disabled, causing a duplex mismatch with the far end.
evidence: R2 running-config (GigabitEthernet0/0/1): "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed": { "value-100" }, "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:duplex": "half", "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation": { "auto": false }
R2 interface operational counters: "input_errors": 115 (shows the symptom on this interface)
R2 interface operational state: "speed": "102400000" (100Mbps), "duplex": null (duplex not negotiated/unknown) — supports mismatch between configured duplex and negotiated state

root_causes.cause: The far-end interface is set to autonegotiation (default), which conflicts with R2's forced settings and results in link negotiation mismatch.
evidence: R1 running-config (GigabitEthernet0/0/1): "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation": { "auto": true } (far end is autonegotiating)

R2 running-config shows autonegotiation disabled and forced speed/duplex (see above), and R2 interface shows input_errors=115 while oper/up — consistent with a mismatch where the other side negotiated differently

plan.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is configured with autonegotiation disabled and forced 100Mbps half-duplex, causing duplex mismatch and input_errors against a far-end that is autonegotiating.
plan.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 and remove forced speed/duplex so both ends negotiate link parameters automatically. Risk: brief link interruption/traffic loss when negotiation changes.

evidence: Latest get_running_config (R2) shows GigabitEthernet0/0/1 with "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation": { "auto": false }, "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed": { "value-100" }, "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:duplex": "half".

Latest get_interface_status (R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1) shows admin_state=up, oper_state=up, speed=102400000 (100Mbps), duplex=null, input_errors=115 — indicating errors and no negotiated duplex reported.
remaining_issues: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 still has autonegotiation disabled and forced speed/duplex (100Mbps half) — configuration mismatch with far end.
Interface operational counters on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 show input_errors (115) and duplex is not negotiated (duplex=null), so the duplex mismatch symptom persists.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is admin/up, oper/up at 100Mbps with input_errors=115 and duplex reported as null (not negotiated).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Duplex/autonegotiation mismatch: R2 is manually set to 100Mbps half-duplex with autonegotiation disabled while the far end is autonegotiating → causes the input errors.

Changes made
- Verified interface is administratively up and re-applied the IP (10.0.0.2/30). No changes were made to negotiation, speed or duplex settings.

Verification
- Result: FAILED.
  - Evidence: R2 running-config still shows negotiation auto=false, speed=100, duplex=half. Interface counters still show input_errors and duplex is not negotiated.
  - Remaining: autonegotiation is still disabled and forced 100/half remains; input_errors persist and duplex is not negotiated.

Recommended next step
- Enable autonegotiation and return speed/duplex to auto on R2 Gi0/0/1 (expect a brief link interruption). Example CLI to run on R2:
  interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1
  negotiation auto
  speed auto
  duplex auto
  (save config)
